texto: Most distinguishable about the idiot, Hedge noted, was their fear of that which was different. Those who feared difference always made a point of finding difference in others in order to feel more secure in their sameness.
explicacao: Citação de Sean DeLauder sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. “Most distinguishable about the idiot, Hedge noted, was their fear of that which was different. Th…” articula reflexao, human-nature, difference em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: Doubt was a giant-sized beast with heavy boots that pounded through the cities of the mind, sparking an occasional life only so Hope could rise again for a splendid recrushing. Doubt reveled in nothing more than destroying Hope. And Hope, being immortal, lived out its days with blithe indifference to its regular obliteration. Neither cared at all about the suffering they caused: Doubt because it enjoyed suffering; Hope because it was too stupid to recognize it.
explicacao: Citação de Sean DeLauder sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Doubt was a giant-sized beast with heavy boots that pounded through the cities of the mind, spark…” articula reflexao, doubt, esperanca em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e esperanca.

Doubt reveled in nothing more than destroying Hope. And Hope, being immortal, lived out its days with blithe indifference to its regular obliteration. texto: Evil became invisible when it was everywhere. Like air, everyone forgot it was there until it was blowing hard enough to knock off their hat or muss up their hair.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sean DeLauder: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Evil became invisible when it was everywhere. Like air, everyone forgot it was there until it was…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, evil, obliviousness pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de obliviousness.
